questions.question:
Which of the following is FALSE ?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 2 | Question: 35
options: The grammar \( S \rightarrow aS|aSbS|\in \), where \( S \) is the only non-terminal symbol, and \( \in \) is the null string, is ambiguous.
An unambiguous grammar has same left most and right most derivation.
An ambiguous grammar can never be \( LR(k) \) for any \( k \).
Recursive descent parser is a top-down parser.

questions.question:
The grammar ‘GI’ \( S \rightarrow OSO \mid ISI \mid 0 \mid 1 \mid \in \) and the grammar G2 is \( S \rightarrow as \mid asb \mid X, X \rightarrow Xa \mid a \).

Which is the correct statement?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 42

Match the following \( : \) 

\( \begin{array} {clcl} & \textbf{List – I} && \textbf{List – II} \\ \text{a.}& \text{Context free grammar} & \text{i.} & \text{Linear bounded automaton} \\  \text{b.}& \text{Regular grammar} & \text{ii.} & \text{Pushdown automaton} \\  \text{c.}& \text{Context sensitive  grammar} & \text{iii.} & \text{Turing machine} \\  \text{d.}& \text{Unrestricted grammar} & \text{iv.} & \text{Deterministic finite automaton} \\  \end{array} \)

\( \textbf{Codes :} \)



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | December 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 62

Consider the following two Grammars:

\( G_1 \: : \: S \rightarrow SbS \mid a \)

\( G_2 : S \rightarrow aB \mid ab, \: A \rightarrow GAB \mid a, \: B \rightarrow ABb \mid b \)

Which one of the folloeing options is correct?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | July 2018 | Part 2 | Question: 36
options: Both \( G_1 \) and \( G_2 \) are not ambiguous

questions.question:
The set \( A = \{ 0^n \: 1^n \: 2^n \mid n=1, 2, 3, \dots \} \) is an example of a grammar that is



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | July 2018 | Part 2 | Question: 38

The equivalent grammar corresponding to the grammar \( G:S \rightarrow aA, A \rightarrow BB, B \rightarrow aBb \mid \varepsilon \) is



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 32
options: \( S \rightarrow aA, A \rightarrow BB, B \rightarrow aBb \)
\( S \rightarrow a \mid aA, A \rightarrow BB, B \rightarrow aBb \mid ab \)
\( S \rightarrow a \mid aA, A \rightarrow BB \mid B, B \rightarrow aBb \)
\( S \rightarrow a \mid aA, A \rightarrow BB \mid B , B \rightarrow aBb \mid ab \)
questions.correct_answer: \( S \rightarrow a \mid aA, A \rightarrow BB \mid B , B \rightarrow aBb \mid ab \)

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are the fundamental semantic model(s) of parameter passing?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 35

Given the following two grammars :

\( G_{1} : S \rightarrow AB | aaB \)

              \( A \rightarrow a | Aa \)

              \( B \rightarrow b \) 

\( G_{2} : S \rightarrow a S b S | b S a S | \lambda \)

Which statement is correct ?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 73
options: \( G_{1} \) is unambiguous and \( G_{2} \) is unambiguous.
\( G_{1} \) is unambiguous and \( G_{2} \) is ambiguous.
\( G_{1} \) is ambiguous and \( G_{2} \) is unambiguous.
\( G_{1} \) is ambiguous and \( G_{2} \) is ambiguous.
questions.correct_answer: \( G_{1} \) is ambiguous and \( G_{2} \) is ambiguous.

questions.question:
Match the following \( : \)

\( \begin{array}{clcl} &  \textbf{List – I} & & \textbf{List – II}  \\   \text{a.} & \text{Chomsky Normal form} & \text{i.} & S \rightarrow b S S \mid a S \mid c \\   \text{b.} & \text{Greibach Normal form} & \text{ii.} & S \rightarrow a S b \mid ab \\  \text{c.} & \text{S-grammar} & \text{iii.} & S \rightarrow AS \mid a  \\  & &  & A \rightarrow SA \mid b \\   \text{d.} & \text{LL grammar} & \text{iv.} & S \rightarrow a B S B  \\  & & & B \rightarrow b \\ \end{array} \)

\( \textbf{Codes :} \)



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 74

If all the production rules have single non-terminal symbol on the left side, the grammar defined is:



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 2 | Question: 33

Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

  1. The grammar \( S \rightarrow SS \mid a \)  is ambiguous. (Where \( S \) is the start symbol)
  2. The grammar \( S \rightarrow 0S1 \mid 01S \mid \epsilon \)  is ambiguous. (The special symbol \( \epsilon \) represents the empty string)  (Where \( S \) is the start symbol)
  3. The grammar (Where \( S \) is the start symbol)
    S -> T/U\( 
T -> x S y | xy | ϵ
U -> yT
    generates a language consisting of the string \)yxxyy$.


Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | November 2017 | Part 2 | Question: 34

Which of the following grammars is(are) ambiguous?

  1. \( s \rightarrow ss \mid asb \mid bsa \mid \lambda \)
  2. \( s \rightarrow asbs \mid bsas \mid \lambda \)
  3. \( s \rightarrow aAB \\ A \rightarrow bBb \\ B \rightarrow A \mid \lambda \text{ where } \lambda \text{ denotes empty string} \)

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 57

LL grammar for the language \( L = \{a^n b^m c^{n+m} \mid m \geq 0, n \geq 0\} \) is



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 16
options: \( S \rightarrow aSc \mid S_1 \lambda \mid S_1 \mid \lambda ; S_1 \rightarrow bS_1c \)
\( S \rightarrow aSc \mid S_1 \lambda \mid S_1 \mid \lambda ; S_1 \rightarrow bS_1c \mid \lambda \)
questions.correct_answer: \( S \rightarrow aSc \mid S_1 \lambda \mid S_1 \mid \lambda ; S_1 \rightarrow bS_1c \mid \lambda \)

questions.question:
Assume the statements \( S_1 \) and \( S_2 \) given as:

\( S_1 \): Given a context free grammar G, there exists an algorithm for determining whether L(G) is infinite.

\( S_2 \): There exists an algorithm to determine whether two context free grammars generate the same language.

Which one of the following is true?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 17
options: \( S_1 \) is correct and \( S_2 \) is not correct
Both \( S_1 \) and \( S_2 \) are not correct
\( S_1 \) is not correct and \( S_2 \) is correct
questions.correct_answer: \( S_1 \) is correct and \( S_2 \) is not correct

questions.question:
Given the following production of a grammar:

\( S \rightarrow aA \mid aBB \);

\( A \rightarrow aaA \mid \lambda \);

\( B \rightarrow bB \mid bbC \);

\( C \rightarrow B \)

Which of the following is true?



Theory of Computation: UGC NET CSE | September 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 18
options: The language corresponding to the given grammar is a set of even number of a’s
The language corresponding to the given grammar is a set of odd number of a’s
The language corresponding to the given grammar is a set of even number of a’s followed by odd number of b’s
The language corresponding to the given grammar is a set of odd number of a’s followed by even number of b’s
questions.correct_answer: The language corresponding to the given grammar is a set of odd number of a’s
